heading: Authorization for use of production flexibility contracts
label: 7211 Authorization for use of production flexibility contracts
notes: References in TextThe Food Security Act of 1985, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), (2), is , , . Subtitles B and C of title XII of the Act are classified generally to subchapters II (§ 3811 et seq.) and III (§ 3821 et seq.), respectively, of chapter 58 of Title 16, Conservation. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 1985 Amendment note set out under and Tables.Pub. (c), was in the original “this subtitle”, meaning subtitle B (§§ 111–118) of title I of , , , which enacted this subchapter and amended sections 1308, 1308–1, and 1308–3 of this title. Violation of Contract; Denial of Funds for Salaries, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–180, title VII, § 726110 Stat. 1600Aug. 6, 1996“None of the funds made available in this Act [see Tables for classification] may be used to pay the salaries of employees of the Department of Agriculture who make payments pursuant to a production flexibility contract entered into under section 111 of the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 (; ) when it is made known to the Federal official having authority to obligate or expend such funds that the land covered by that production flexibility contract is not being used for the production of an agricultural commodity or is not devoted to a conserving use, unless it is also made known to that Federal official that the lack of agricultural production or the lack of a conserving use is a consequence of drought, flood, or other natural disaster.”Public Law 104–1277 U.S.C. 7211

children.text: comply with applicable conservation requirements under subtitle B of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 3811
comply with applicable wetland protection requirements under subtitle C of title XII of the Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 3821
comply with the planting flexibility requirements of ; andsection 7218 of this title
use the land subject to the contract for an agricultural or related activity, but not for a nonagricultural commercial or industrial use, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Eligible owners and producers described
children.label: b Eligible owners and producers described

children.text: An owner of eligible cropland who assumes all or a part of the risk of producing a crop.
A producer (other than an owner) on eligible cropland with a share-rent lease of the eligible cropland, regardless of the length of the lease, if the owner enters into the same contract.

A producer (other than an owner) on eligible cropland who cash rents the eligible cropland under a lease expiring on or after , in which case the owner is not required to enter into the contract.September 30, 2002

A producer (other than an owner) on eligible cropland who cash rents the eligible cropland under a lease expiring before . The owner of the eligible cropland may also enter into the same contract. If the producer elects to enroll less than 100 percent of the eligible cropland in the contract, the consent of the owner is required.September 30, 2002

An owner of eligible cropland who cash rents the eligible cropland and the lease term expires before , if the tenant declines to enter into a contract. In the case of an owner covered by this paragraph, contract payments shall not begin under a contract until the lease held by the tenant ends.September 30, 2002
An owner or producer described in any preceding paragraph regardless of whether the owner or producer purchased catastrophic risk protection for a 1996 crop under .section 1508(b) of this title

children.heading: Tenants and sharecroppers
children.label: c Tenants and sharecroppers
children.text: In carrying out this subchapter, the Secretary shall provide adequate safeguards to protect the interests of tenants and sharecroppers.

for at least 1 of the 1991 through 1995 crops, at least a portion of the land was enrolled in the acreage reduction program authorized for a crop of a contract commodity under section 101B, 103B, 105B, or 107B of the Agricultural Act of 1949 or was considered planted;
was subject to a conservation reserve contract under section 1231 of the Food Security Act of 1985 () whose term expired, or was voluntarily terminated, on or after ; or16 U.S.C. 3831January 1, 1995
is released from coverage under a conservation reserve contract by the Secretary during the period beginning on , and ending on the date specified in .January 1, 1995section 7212(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Quantity of eligible cropland covered by contract
children.label: e Quantity of eligible cropland covered by contract
children.text: Subject to subsection (b)(4), an owner or producer may enroll as contract acreage all or a portion of the eligible cropland on the farm.

children.heading: Voluntary reduction in contract acreage
children.label: f Voluntary reduction in contract acreage
children.text: Subject to subsection (b)(4), an owner or producer who enters into a contract may subsequently reduce the quantity of contract acreage covered by the contract.
